task_completion.description: Configure the 5 active Port engine tags in the Data Tags section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Crimson 3.0 Data Tags navigation pane or main configuration window, the 5 expected Port tags (PT_OP_101, PT_CT_101, PT_EA_101, PT_EB_101, PT_SP_101) should be visible and configured.

task_completion.description: Ensure no Starboard (ST_*) tags are configured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data Tags navigation pane, there should be no tags starting with 'ST_' (such as ST_OP_201, ST_CT_201, etc.) present in the list.

task_completion.description: Create a continuous Data Logger named 'PortEngineLog'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data Logger section of the Navigation Pane, a new data logger explicitly named 'PortEngineLog' should be visible.

task_completion.description: Set the Data Logger update rate to 1 second.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With 'PortEngineLog' selected, the properties pane should show the 'Update Rate' setting configured to '1 Second'.

task_completion.description: Add all 5 Port engine tags to the Data Logger.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Contents or Data section of the 'PortEngineLog' configuration, the 5 Port engine tags (PT_OP_101, PT_CT_101, PT_EA_101, PT_EB_101, PT_SP_101) should be listed as the items being logged.

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'marine_engine.c3' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Crimson 3.0 application title bar should display 'marine_engine.c3', or a 'Save As' dialog screenshot should show the file being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\marine_engine.c3'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Red Lion Crimson 3.0 GUI to perform configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Crimson 3.0 interface being actively used (menus open, fields being typed into, panes selected) rather than just a final state appearing instantly.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts or text editors to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no PowerShell, Command Prompt, or text editor windows open modifying the .c3 file directly.

integrity.description: The project was saved using the application's built-in save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the File menu 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog being used, confirming the file was generated by the software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Port engine tags to be configured: PT_OP_101, PT_CT_101, PT_EA_101, PT_EB_101, PT_SP_101. Forbidden Starboard tags: Any tag starting with 'ST_' (e.g., ST_OP_201, ST_CT_201, ST_EA_201, ST_EB_201, ST_SP_201). The Data Logger must be named exactly 'PortEngineLog' with an Update Rate of '1 Second'. The final save path must be 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\marine_engine.c3'.
